name: Labor Colony
description:
Labor Colony — Prefab Worker Accommodation for 50 to 500+

A labor colony from SAMAN is a fully planned, factory-built worker accommodation infrastructure — not a collection of individual cabins. We supply complete colony setups for EPC contractors, highway and dam project firms, power plant operators, and large factory setup teams across India, from our manufacturing facilities in Bangalore and Greater Noida.

If your project employs 10 or more construction workers, worker accommodation near the site is not optional — it is a legal requirement. This page covers capacity planning, colony infrastructure, project types, deployment timeline, and asset recovery at project end.

Your Legal Obligation Before the First Worker Arrives

Section 34 of the Building and Other Construction Workers Act 1996 (BOCW Act) requires every employer of 10 or more construction workers to provide free temporary accommodation near the work site — with separate cooking area, bathing facilities, washing provisions, and latrine and urinal accommodation.

This is not a welfare gesture. It is a statutory obligation. For EPC contractors bidding on government highway, dam, and power projects, BOCW Act compliance is a contract condition. Non-compliance carries fines, site closure orders, and disqualification from future tenders.

Every SAMAN labor colony is designed to meet BOCW Act Section 34 accommodation standards — dormitories, covered cooking area, washroom blocks, and sanitation facilities are all part of the standard colony layout. When your inspector arrives, your accommodation is compliant.

How Many Workers — Planning Your Colony Capacity

Before ordering, confirm your worker count and available land area. These two numbers determine everything else.

50 workers: 5 to 7 dormitory units of 10 persons each, 2 to 3 washroom blocks, 1 canteen unit. Land requirement approximately 30m × 20m on flat ground.

100 workers: 10 to 12 dormitory units, 4 washroom blocks, 1 canteen, 1 supervisor quarters. Land approximately 50m × 25m.

200 workers: 20 to 25 dormitory units, 6 to 8 washroom blocks, 2 canteen units, 2 supervisor quarters, 1 medical room provision. Land approximately 80m × 40m.

300 to 500 workers: Full colony layout with stacked double-storey dormitory blocks where land is constrained. Canteen scaled to serve 2 meal sittings. Phased delivery and assembly in 3 to 4 deployment batches.

SAMAN double-storey labor colony dormitory block — two-floor stacked configuration with steel staircases and balcony walkways. Standard configuration for 300 to 500 worker colonies where land area is constrained. Delivered and assembled across India in 10 days.

These are planning estimates. Your actual layout is confirmed during site assessment before order. For full facility specifications per capacity level, see our labor accommodations subpage.

What a Complete Colony Includes — Beyond Just Dormitories

Most suppliers sell individual units. SAMAN supplies complete, functional labor colony infrastructure.

A full SAMAN labor colony covers: dormitory sleeping blocks, washroom and toilet blocks meeting BOCW Act sanitation ratios, a canteen unit with cooking provisions, supervisor and engineer quarters separate from general worker dormitories, a site security post, and a general store or materials room.

You confirm your worker count, land dimensions, and project duration. We provide the colony layout drawing, unit count per facility type, delivery batch sequence, and assembly schedule — all before order confirmation.

For a completely planned, factory-made colony setup covering all facility types, our labor camps page covers the full turnkey configuration. For buyers who need factory-fabricated units with coordinated delivery across all facility types, see our prefab labor camps page.

Infrastructure Projects This Colony Is Built For

SAMAN multi-block labor colony at an infrastructure project site — two double-storey dormitory blocks in green and white, labeled for block identification. Standard configuration for power plant and industrial construction projects with 150 to 400 workers over 18 to 36-month durations.

Highway and road projects (100 to 300 workers): Extended linear sites where the workforce moves along the project corridor. Colony is set up at the primary camp location and supplemented with satellite shelters along the route.

Dam and tunnel projects (200 to 500 workers): High worker density at a single location, often in hilly or remote terrain. Double-storey dormitory stacking is standard to reduce land footprint. Access coordination is confirmed before dispatch.

Power plant and industrial construction (150 to 400 workers): Mix of skilled and unskilled accommodation — separate supervisor quarters with a higher fitout standard alongside standard worker dormitories. Long project durations of 18 to 36 months make purchase significantly more economical than rental.

Factory setup and industrial expansion (50 to 200 workers): Shorter duration of 6 to 18 months. Single-storey colony on factory land. Often reused on the next facility expansion immediately after.

For buyers from EPC firms needing a fully planned colony with all facilities coordinated, our prefab labour colony page covers that turnkey arrangement.

Setup in 10 Days — How a Full Colony is Deployed

A SAMAN labor colony is assembled on site — not constructed. The difference matters for your project timeline.

Units are fabricated at our Bangalore or Greater Noida facility, tested, and dispatched in coordinated batches aligned to your site readiness. The first batch typically covers dormitory blocks and washrooms — the minimum occupancy requirement. Canteen and supervisor units arrive in the second batch. Full colony commissioning completes within 10 working days for standard accessible sites of up to 200 workers.

Site preparation required from your end: level ground at each unit placement position, temporary electrical connection point, and water supply connection point. SAMAN handles transport, placement, structural assembly, and electrical connection across all units.

For colonies above 300 workers, the timeline extends to 15 to 18 working days due to batch sequencing — phased worker arrival aligned to phased colony completion is standard practice for large projects.

Remote and Difficult Terrain — What We Handle Differently

Highway, dam, and tunnel projects are frequently in the hills of Himachal Pradesh and Uttarakhand, the deserts of Rajasthan, the forests of Chhattisgarh and Odisha, or coastal sites in Gujarat and Andhra Pradesh. Flat, accessible industrial land is not the default for infrastructure projects.

SAMAN units are designed for modular transport — each unit is sized to fit standard flat-bed trucks and can be broken into smaller transport components for sites accessible only by narrow mountain roads. Assembly is by bolt-and-panel — no welding on site.

Deployed SAMAN labor colony dormitory block at an industrial infrastructure project site — double-storey layout with multiple rooms per floor, external staircase, and open courtyard area. Colony infrastructure includes sleeping blocks, washrooms, canteen, and supervisor quarters as standard.

For remote sites, we confirm access road width, load capacity, and crane availability before confirming the delivery schedule. Where crane access is not available, lighter unit formats are specified. For buyers who need early-stage temporary cover during colony construction at a remote site, our labor shelters page covers fast-deploy temporary shelter units.

Colony Asset Recovery — What Happens When the Project Ends

A SAMAN labor colony is a redeployable capital asset — not a writeoff at project end.

Standard dormitory and facility units can be dismantled by a crew of 4 to 6 people in 3 to 5 days per block. Units are loaded onto transport, moved to the storage yard or directly to the next project site, and reassembled. Structural integrity is maintained through 3 to 5 full deployment cycles under standard handling conditions.

For an EPC contractor running back-to-back highway or power projects, the amortised per-project cost of purchasing a colony across 3 deployments is typically 30 to 40% lower than renting equivalent accommodation for the same total period. The asset sits on your balance sheet, depreciates over its working life, and moves with your project portfolio.

For projects under 8 months where purchase is not justified, rental configurations are available. Call or WhatsApp +91 97089 89937 to confirm rental availability for your project timeline.

Which Colony Unit Does Your Project Need

The 10 subpages in this cluster cover every colony component and configuration type — organised by function:

Sleeping accommodation: labor hutments — standard worker sleeping units · labor cottages — individual cottage-style units for longer stays · labor sheds — shed-format housing for large numbers · prefab labor sheds — factory-made shed units · prefab labor hutments — consistent factory-made hutment units

SAMAN labor colony deployed on project land — two-storey dormitory block and single-storey canteen unit visible in the same colony footprint. Colony units are dismantled in 3 to 5 days per block and redeployed to the next project, reducing per-project accommodation cost by 30 to 40% compared to rental.

Complete colony setups: labor camps · prefab labor camps · prefab labour colony · labor accommodations · labor shelters

Frequently Asked Questions

Does a SAMAN labor colony meet BOCW Act Section 34 accommodation requirements?

Yes. Every SAMAN labor colony layout includes dormitory sleeping accommodation, separate cooking area, bathing and washing facilities, and latrine and urinal blocks — the four specific requirements under BOCW Act Section 34. Layout drawings confirming facility provision are supplied with every colony order for BOCW inspection purposes.

What is the minimum worker count for a labor colony quote from SAMAN?

SAMAN provides colony quotes for projects housing 50 workers or more. For smaller site accommodation requirements of 10 to 50 workers, our prefabricated houses range covers bunkhouse and site accommodation units. The labor colony cluster is sized for 50+ worker projects with full facility infrastructure.

Does SAMAN handle the site layout planning or do we need to provide a layout?

SAMAN provides the colony layout drawing as part of the quotation process. You provide your worker count, available land dimensions, and project duration. We confirm the unit count per facility type, placement sequence, and delivery batch schedule. You do not need to commission a separate layout designer.

What documentation does SAMAN provide for tender submissions requiring worker accommodation details?

Every SAMAN labor colony order includes a layout drawing with unit dimensions and placement, a facility list confirming dormitory capacity, sanitation ratios, and canteen provision, and a factory inspection certificate for all units. These documents support BOCW Act compliance verification and tender submission requirements.

How long do SAMAN labor colony units last across multiple project deployments?

Standard SAMAN dormitory and facility units maintain structural integrity through 4 to 5 complete deployment cycles under normal handling. With anti-corrosion treatment and standard maintenance between deployments, units have a working life of 10 to 15 years — typically 3 to 5 major projects depending on project duration.

Tell us your worker count, project type, site location, and required setup date.

We will confirm layout, unit count, delivery schedule, and pricing within 24 hours — for both purchase and rental configurations.

Call or WhatsApp: +91 97089 89937

For smaller site accommodation needs of up to 20 people, see our prefabricated houses range — bunkhouse and site accommodation units without the full colony infrastructure.
